metadata.note: Some structural features are manually sourced from published reports. Items marked [CITATION NEEDED] require verification before final publication.

retail_participation.detail: Number of online brokerage accounts grew from 1.5M (1997) to 9.7M (2000)
retail_participation.source: [CITATION NEEDED - SEC/FINRA data on online brokerage growth]

leverage.detail: NYSE margin debt peaked at $278.5B in March 2000, up from $141B in 1997

public_float.detail: 486 IPOs in 1999, 406 in 2000. Average first-day return 71% in 1999

speculative_disconnect.detail: Median P/E of NASDAQ stocks exceeded 100 at peak; many IPOs had no revenue

rapid_unwind.detail: NASDAQ fell 78% from peak (5,048) to trough (1,114) over 31 months

retail_participation.detail: Homeownership rate peaked at 69.2% (2004). Subprime loans ~20% of originations by 2006
retail_participation.source: US Census Bureau; Inside Mortgage Finance

leverage.detail: Average LTV exceeded 80%; many loans 100%+ LTV. CDO notional values in trillions

public_float.detail: MBS widely held by pension funds, banks, retail investors globally

denial.detail: 'Housing prices never decline nationally' was consensus. Bernanke (2005): 'no bubble'

speculative_disconnect.detail: Home prices 70% above historical trend relative to rents and income

rapid_unwind.detail: S&P 500 fell 57% from Oct 2007 to Mar 2009. Cascading bank failures, AIG bailout

retail_participation.detail: Japanese household stock ownership peaked; land speculation widespread among individuals
retail_participation.source: [CITATION NEEDED - Bank of Japan financial statistics]

leverage.detail: Bank lending grew ~10% annually 1985-1989. Land used as collateral for further land purchases

public_float.detail: Tokyo Stock Exchange was world's largest by market cap in 1989. Highly liquid public markets
public_float.source: World Federation of Exchanges historical data

denial.detail: 'Japan as Number One' narrative. Imperial Palace grounds worth more than all California real estate
denial.source: Vogel (1979), Japan as Number One; widely cited comparison

speculative_disconnect.detail: Nikkei P/E exceeded 70 at peak. Land prices 5-10x reasonable valuations
speculative_disconnect.source: [CITATION NEEDED - BOJ land price survey data]

rapid_unwind.detail: Nikkei fell ~60% by 1992 but full recovery took 30+ years (slow deflation, not panic crash)

cycle_2017.note: BTC price from CoinMarketCap historical data

cycle_2021.note: BTC price from CoinMarketCap historical data

retail_participation.detail: Coinbase had 98M verified users by Q4 2021. NFT market dominated by retail

leverage.detail: Significant DeFi leverage and exchange margin trading, but less than traditional finance

public_float.detail: Crypto markets operate 24/7 with near-instant settlement. Highly liquid for BTC/ETH

denial.detail: Crypto community actively trained members to dismiss 'FUD'. But external skepticism was high

speculative_disconnect.detail: NFT market reached $25B in 2021 sales. Many projects had no utility beyond speculation

rapid_unwind.detail: BTC fell 77% (2021 cycle). NFT floor prices collapsed 90%+. FTX/Luna/3AC cascading failures
rapid_unwind.source: Market data; public reporting on FTX collapse
